incorrects.description: Student selected 6 — only counted Deer, missed Birds

params.description: Both Birds and Deer rows pulse to show both categories need to be counted

steps.text: You counted just one category. The question asks for Birds AND Deer in all—count both rows together.

incorrects.description: Student selected 7 — only counted Birds, missed Deer

params.description: Both Birds and Deer rows pulse to show both categories need to be counted

steps.text: You counted just one category. The question asks for Birds AND Deer in all—count both rows, then add them together.

incorrects.description: Student selected 16 — likely counted all categories instead of just Birds and Deer

params.description: Only Birds and Deer rows remain highlighted, other categories dim

steps.text: You counted all the animals. We only need Birds and Deer. Count those two rows: Birds has 7, Deer has 6.

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly — system models the correct answer

params.description: System counts Birds row (7 symbols light up one by one), then Deer row (6 symbols light up), then shows 7 + 6 = 13

steps.text: Let me show you how this works. First, count the Birds row: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7. Then count the Deer row: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6. Now add them: 7 plus 6 equals 13. That's how many in all.
